name: ansible-runner (Python API for Ansible)

performance.notes: Depends on playbook complexity and target host count

agent_readiness.notes: Stable Python API for running Ansible. import ansible_runner. r = ansible_runner.run(private_data_dir='/tmp/runner', playbook='site.yml'). r.status, r.rc. Async: ansible_runner.run_async(). Event streaming: for event in r.events. Thread-safe. AWX/Tower and AAP (Ansible Automation Platform) use ansible-runner internally. Directory structure: project/ (playbooks), inventory/ (hosts), env/ (passwords, extravars). Event-driven progress monitoring. Compared to direct ansible Python API: stable and supported.

gotchas: Directory structure (private_data_dir) has specific layout requirements
Errors returned as status='failed' — need to parse events for details
Event parsing for granular progress tracking is complex
best_when: Running Ansible playbooks programmatically from Python applications
Building automation platforms on top of Ansible
AWX-compatible automation (runner is AWX's execution backend)
Event-driven Ansible execution with streaming progress
avoid_when: Simple CLI use (ansible-playbook command is simpler)
summary: ansible-runner provides a stable Python API for Ansible execution. ansible_runner.run(private_data_dir='/tmp', playbook='site.yml') — r.status, r.rc, event streaming. Used internally by AWX and Ansible Automation Platform. Async support. Thread-safe. Preferred over direct Ansible Python API for programmatic use. Specific directory structure (project/, inventory/, env/) required.
